subject: Statistics
title: Personalized Medicine
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Personalized Medicine.

hook.text: A drug works for 40% of patients. What if you could predict which 40% — and give it only to them?

buildup.text: Personalized medicine uses genetics, biomarkers, and patient data to tailor treatments to individuals.

discovery.text: Pharmacogenomics tests which genetic variants affect drug metabolism. The same dose helps some and harms others.

twist.text: Average treatment effects hide enormous individual variation. The average patient doesn't exist.

climax.text: Statistics is shifting from 'does this drug work on average?' to 'does this drug work for you?'

punchline.text: The right drug for the right patient. Statistics makes it personal.

quiz.question: What does personalized medicine aim to do?
options: Predict which specific patients will benefit from a treatment using genetics and biomarkers
Give every patient the same standard treatment
Eliminate the need for clinical trials
